stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Easter truce expires as both sides accuse the other of violations
stories.summary: Ukraine records more than 2,000 violations, Russia claims 1,900. Zelenskyy congratulates Hungary’s Péter Magyar. What we know on day 1,510 A ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine to mark the Orthodox Easter formally expired on Monday , with both sides having accused each other of thousands of violations, despite a lull in Russian air raids. The truce lasted 32 hours, from 4pm (1300 GMT) on Saturday until the end of the day on Sunday. Both sides had agreed to observe the ceasefire, which the Russi

stories.title: Monday briefing: Hungary chooses Péter Magyar over Viktor Orbán
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power, ushering in a new era for Hungary’s relations with the EU, US and Russia Good morning. The people of Hungary are waking up in an unfamiliar political landscape – one in which Viktor Orbán, who has served as prime minister since 2010, is stepping aside after defeat to Péter Magyar, whose Tisza party has won an election likely to reshape the country’s ties with the EU, the US and Russia. Less than three hours after polls closed

stories.title: Bulgaria’s elections are not for sale, PM insists as he vows crackdown on tampering
stories.summary: Bulgaria’s Prime Minister Andrey Gurov is promising tougher action against vote-buying and election manipulation as the country heads into its eighth parliamentary election in five years. The vote follows mass protests in December over accusations of entrenched government corruption and backsliding on the rule of law that toppled the center-right GERB-led coalition. Gurov, a deputy governor for the central bank and member of the anti-corruption party Continuing the Change, was appointed interim

stories.title: New UK steel tariffs threaten British manufacturing, industry warns
stories.summary: LONDON — New protections for the U.K. steel sector will fuel inflation and threaten thousands of jobs throughout the manufacturing sector, metal importers are warning the British government. The protections “risk severely disrupting supply chains” serving Britain’s automotive, construction, infrastructure and engineering sectors, Confederation of British Metalforming President Stephen Morley said in a letter to Trade Minister Chris Bryant, shared with POLITICO. Ministers announced last month tha

stories.title: DHS investigating claim about Swalwell nanny filed by conspiracy theorist
stories.summary: Joel Gilbert, who mailed anti-Barack Obama film to voters in 2012, accuses congressman of violating immigration law Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox California Democrat Eric Swalwell’s decision to suspend his campaign for governor on Sunday, even as he denies allegations from four women who accuse him of sexual misconduct and assault, did not end the pressure the congressman faces. One sexual assault allegation against Swalwell, alleged to have been c

stories.title: Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 'painful' election result - video
stories.summary: Orbán concedes defeat after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' election result, less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday. 'I congratulated the victorious party,' the rightwing populist told supporters in Budapest after a landslide win by Péter Magyar’s Tisza party. 'We are going to serve the Hungarian nation and our homeland from opposition as well,' he said Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Hungary has chosen Europe’: EU leaders jubilant after Péter Magyar’s victory over Orbán
stories.summary: Congratulations pour in from across EU, with leaders from Spain, Poland, France, Britain, Denmark, Romania, Sweden and beyond hailing a new chapter Full report: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power EU leaders heaped praise on Péter Magyar after his decisive election victory in Hungary against the long-serving prime minister Viktor Orbán, who many saw as a direct threat to Europe’s peace and prosperity. The outpouring reflected a deep frustration with Orbán across the E

stories.title: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary’s election sends a jolting message — to US Democrats
stories.summary: The defeat of Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, should deliver a sharp jolt to one of America’s two major political parties. Oddly, it’s not the Republicans, deeply invested though they were in Orbán as a fellow traveler. There is no question that Orbán’s downfall is a loss for MAGA-style politics and a reminder that even a developed system of so-called “illiberal democracy” has its limits. President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance committed personal credibility and political capi

stories.title: Péter Magyar: Hungary’s next leader energised voters but is ‘a dark horse’
stories.summary: Ex-government insider turned Orbán critic is authentic and passionate but his proposed policies are short on details As a child growing up in Budapest, Péter Magyar had a poster of Viktor Orbán – at the time a leading figure in the country’s pro-democracy movement – hanging above his bed. Orbán was one of several political figures who adorned his bedroom, Magyar told a podcast last year, hinting at his excitement over the changes sweeping the country after the collapse of communism. Now Magyar,

stories.title: Magyar celebrates as Orbán’s 16-year rule ends - as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. You can read more about election night here Europe correspondent Not a regular observer of Hungarian politics? We’ve got you. Continue reading...

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, international isolation and oligarchs amassing wealth. His landslide

stories.title: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power
stories.summary: Péter Magyar’s Tisza party wins election as prime minister concedes defeat, in result likely to reshape ties with EU ‘Hungary has chosen Europe’: EU leaders jubilant after Magyar’s victory Hungary’s opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, has won the election, bringing an end to Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power, in a result that is likely to rattle the White House and reshape the country’s relationship with the EU. Less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday, Orbán conceded defea

stories.title: Magyar set to outline Hungary plans after resounding victory over Orbán – Europe live
stories.summary: Winner’s Tisza party to control more than two-thirds of new parliament, offering chance for a complete break with changes wrought by illiberal regime Good morning from Budapest , as the city reluctantly and not without some difficulties wakes up from the celebrations the night before, which went on to 5am and beyond. With 98.94% of votes counted, Péter Magyar ’s Tisza party is projected to get 138 seats in the new parliament, with just 55 for Viktor Orbán’s Fidesz and six for the far-right Mi Ha

stories.title: Hungary: Orban's ouster heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Hungarian voters have chosen the pro-EU course of Peter Magyar, ending 16 years of rule by Russia-friendly Viktor Orban. Europe is now waiting to see if he can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
stories.summary: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

stories.title: Orbán loses, Magyar wins, Europe reacts — live updates
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán was ousted as prime minister of Hungary after Péter Magyar won a stunning victory in the national election on Sunday. Longtime leader Orbán, Europe’s most prominent populist-nationalist politician, had frustrated Brussels’ agenda on multiple files, including a €90B loan to help Ukraine in its fight against Russia. After 16 years in power, Orbán’s exit is set to change EU dynamics. Follow all the reaction below to Orbán’s loss, as Brussels gears up for the new

stories.title: Record turnout expected as parliamentary election in Hungary gets under way
stories.summary: Polls opened at 6am across the country, and will close 7pm local time. So far, all figures point to a record turnout.

stories.title: Seismic shift: How 16 years of Hungarian politics changed in one day
stories.summary: The National Election Commission said voter turnout was at a record 77.8%, the highest ever recorded in a Hungarian election.

stories.title: Orbán and Magyar vote in high-stakes Hungarian election watched by EU
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán and Péter Magyar vote in a pivotal Hungarian election with record turnout expected, as the result could reshape Budapest’s role in the EU.

stories.title: Vége van: 16 év után megbukott Orbán Viktor rendszere, Magyar Péter alakíthat kormányt
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt kétharmados többséget szerzett a választásokon, Magyar Péter lesz az új miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Lemond Dobrev és a DK teljes elnöksége
stories.summary: Azt mondta, hogy a felelősség innen Magyaré, és reméli, hogy a Tisza elnöke nem viseli tovább a bocskait, mert megosztónak tartja.

stories.title: Iványi Gábor: Ennek a képmutató, embertelen politikának véget kell vetni
stories.summary: Az Orbán Viktor idősebb gyermekeit egykor megkeresztelő lelkész reményei szerint véget ér az a politikai világ, ahol az MNB 600 milliárdját büntetlenül el lehet lopni.

stories.title: After sixteen years, Orbán's regime is over, Péter Magyar set to form government
stories.summary: The Tisza Party has won a two-thirds majority in Hungary's election, which Péter Magyar said they would use to restore Hungary to being a democratic state governed by the rule of law.

stories.title: Magyarország Európát választotta – sorban gratulálnak Magyar Péternek az európai vezetők és államfők
stories.summary: A német kancellár, a francia elnök, az Európai Bizottság elnöke és a brit miniszterelnök is kifejezte jókívánságait.

stories.title: Zelenszkij is gratulált Magyar Péternek
stories.summary: „Készen állunk a találkozókra és a közös konstruktív munkára mindkét nemzet javára”-írta az ukrán elnök az X-en.

stories.title: „Óráid vannak hátra, Orbán!” – kiáltotta valaki 2018-ban a köztévén. De mennyi is lett az pontosan?
stories.summary: 2018-ban valaki bekiabált a köztévé élő adásába Londonban, a jelenet azóta legendás lett. Választ kaptunk a kettővel ezelőtti választás legnagyobb kérdésére!

stories.title: Magyar Péter győzelmi beszédében elszámoltatást és megbékélést ígért, és lemondásra szólította fel Sulyok Tamást
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke szerint minél hamarabb át akarják venni a kormányzást, ami után úgy akarnak majd kormányozni, hogy azzal a fideszesek is elégedettek legyenek. A kétharmados felhatalmazást elmondása szerint egy „működő és emberséges” Magyarország létrehozására akarják fordítani.

stories.title: Donald Tusk is üdvözölte a Tisza győzelmét
stories.summary: Magyarország-Lengyelország-EU újra együtt! – írta a lengyel miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Mráz Ágoston a köztévén arról értekezett, hogy Magyar győzelme egy flash mob, egy villámcsődület, ami egy pillanatra összeállt
stories.summary: Az M1 műsorában sokáig nem merték megmutatni a teljes állást, de Sárvár történelméről meg a szarvasbőgésről is beszélgettek.

stories.title: Így nézett ki a nap, amikor a Tisza Párt elsöpörte Orbán Viktor 16 éve fennálló rendszerét
stories.summary: 2026. április 12-e, Magyarország 10. szabad választása óriási ellenzéki győzelmet hozott. Fotósaink legjobb képeivel mutatjuk be, hogy zajlott a nap a két versengő pártnál, és hogyan ünnepelte Budapest Magyar Péterék diadalát.

stories.title: „Történelmi hatalomváltás Magyarországon” – így reagált a nemzetközi sajtó a Tisza győzelmére
stories.summary: Londontól New Yorkig, Madridtól Moszkváig címlapokon és kiemelt helyen számoltak be a külföldi lapok Orbán bukásáról.

stories.title: A remény felzabálta a félelmet
stories.summary: Magyar Péter két év alatt meggyőzte az embereket arról, hogy nem kell félniük, és hogy a Fidesz által kidolgozott rendszerben is le lehet váltani Orbán Viktor rendszerét.

stories.title: – Hogy van? – Szarul! – Sírtak és lökdösődtek a Fidesz veresége után
stories.summary: Szinte senki sem számított a Fidesz-szimpatizánsok közül a kormánypártok történelmi vereségére, az okokat még keresik. Mutatjuk mi történt a fideszes eredményvárón, miután Orbán Viktor bejelentette, hogy a Fidesz elveszítette az országgyűlési választást.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs: A választási eredmény világos és egyértelmű
stories.summary: Ukrajna már nem kéri az állampolgáraitól, hogy kerüljék el Magyarországot. Orbán Viktor szövetségese, Aleksandar Vučić szerb elnök is gratulált Magyar Péternek. A választás másnapja percről percre.

stories.title: A Mi Hazánk nem lett a mérleg nyelve, de több szavazatot szerzett, mint négy éve
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk valószínűleg újra hat mandátumhoz jut, arányaiban szinte ugyanannyi szavazója volt, mint 2022-ben, de összességében bő nyolcezer fővel bővült a tábora.

stories.title: Nulla pénzből is folytatja a Magyar Kétfarkú Kutya Párt
stories.summary: „Ne utáljátok azt, aki hatalomra került. Kritizálni lehet” – mondta a választási eredményekről Nagy Dávid, a Kutyapárt listavezetője a szimpatizánsoknak a párt eredményváróján.

stories.title: „A győzelmünk nem a Holdról, hanem minden magyar ablakból látszik”
stories.summary: „A kilencvenes választás sem volt ekkora eufória” – mondta egy Tisza-szavazó, miután pártja tizenhat év után legyőzte a Fideszt. A Tisza legboldogabb politikusa egyértelműen Hegedűs Zsolt, a párt egészségügyi szakértője volt.

stories.title: Felrobbant Budapest a Tisza győzelme után
stories.summary: 16 év után másfél óra alatt vége lett Orbán Viktor kormányzásának. Feszültség, örömkönnyek és fesztelen tombolás a választás éjszakáján a fővárosban.

stories.title: The day the Tisza Party swept away Viktor Orbán’s 16-year regime
stories.summary: On April 12, 2026, Hungary’s 10th free election resulted in a landslide victory for the opposition. Through some of the best shots of our photographers, here’s a look at how the day unfolded for the two contending parties and how Budapest celebrated Péter Magyar’s triumph.

stories.title: Bódi Mátyás: Harmadszorra riogatta háborúval a Fidesz a választókat, ez mostanra fulladt ki
stories.summary: A Választási Földrajz szakértője szerint ami 2022-ben bejött, az 2026-ban már nem volt elég. A Fidesz mozgósítása nem volt rossz, de nem volt elég, Magyar Péter pedig népszavazás szintjére emelte a kampányt arról, hogy kelethez vagy nyugathoz akarunk-e tartozni.

stories.title: Ukraine no longer advises its citizens to avoid Hungary
stories.summary: The Ukrainian Foreign Ministry said that the Hungarian people's decision has demonstrated that "our neighbours desire to live n a truly independent Hungary that is part of a united and free Europe".

stories.title: Erős mínuszban indult a kormányközeli tőzsdei cégeknek a választás utáni reggel
stories.summary: Kétharmados győzelmet ért el a vasárnapi országgyűlési választáson a Tisza Párt, nagy mozgásokkal reagált a magyar tőzsde.

stories.title: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint

stories.title: Magyar Péter hallucináló állampártról beszélt, és közben bizakodva várja az eredményt
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke szerint a megfigyelőik is hatékonyak voltak, mert szerinte kevesebb szavazatot tudott most vásárolni a Fidesz.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor elismerte a választási vereséget: Mi ellenzékből is a hazánkat fogjuk szolgálni
stories.summary: Élete legrövidebb beszédét mondta a leköszönő miniszterelnök.

stories.title: A legfontosabb választási eredmények röviden, egy helyen
stories.summary: Minden korábbinál nagyobb felhatalmazást kaptak Magyar Péterék vasárnap, a Fidesz legerősebb választókerületeit is átfordították.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.title: Hungarians speak to the Guardian before decisive election – video
stories.summary: Voters will head to the polls on Sunday in what is seen as Europe’s most consequential election of the year. The Guardian's Jakub Krupa and Flora Garamvolgyi speak to Hungarians in Budapest before the vote Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
stories.summary: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

stories.title: Elections in Hungary: What do the polls say?
stories.summary: Medián, currently considered among the most accurate pollsters in Hungary, predicts a two-thirds majority in parliament for opposition party Tisza. Most other pollsters estimate a smaller lead for them, while few have the ruling Fidesz party narrowly ahead.

stories.title: Hungary Elections: There is hope that Peter Magyar can deliver a new beginning
stories.summary: What will Hungary’s elections bring? In an op-ed for Euronews, EU politician Valérie Hayer expresses hope that Péter Magyar could usher in a new political beginning, while warning that another term for Viktor Orbán risks cementing irreversible democratic backsliding.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: The Iran war has been good for Starmer — to a point
stories.summary: Mujtaba Rahman is the head of Eurasia Group’s Europe practice. He posts at @Mij_Europe . The war in Iran has improved U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s chances of remaining in office. Make no mistake, his underlying position is still weak. Yet as the tenuous ceasefire looks like it may unravel, the likelihood of Starmer facing a leadership challenge after the May 7 regional elections to the Scottish and Welsh parliaments and English councils is falling. In fact, Labour members of parliame

stories.title: ‘Weak on Crime, Weak on Nuclear Weapons’: Trump attacks Pope Leo
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump blasted Pope Leo XIV on Monday, accusing the pontiff of being “WEAK on Crime” and “terrible for Foreign Policy” in a Truth Social post that underscores a widening rift between the White House and the Vatican. Trump’s 334-word screed followed recent remarks by Leo condemning the Israeli-U.S. war in Iran, including describing a threat by Trump to strike Iranian civilian infrastructure as “truly unacceptable.” “God does not bless any conflict,” Leo wrote on X Friday. “An

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US blockade of Iran’s ports to begin later today as Trump says he doesn’t care about further talks
stories.summary: Centcom says blockade to begin at 10am ET; US president says ‘I don’t care if they come back or not’ Full report: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail Planeloads of negotiators and too little time: US and Iran’s 21 hours of talks Circling back to Donald Trump’s coming naval blockade, the US military said it would block all Iranian Gulf ports on Monday at 10am ET on Monday (5.30pm in Iran and 1400 GMT) , effectively seizing control of maritime traffic in the st

stories.title: Australia news live: Barnaby Joyce says criminals need ‘redemption’ after Pauline Hanson sacks convicted rapist; Labor ‘not considering’ joining US blockade of strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Government to fund ads for helpline on pornography websites The child abuse prevention service is operated by Jesuit Social Services , which has run a trial scheme since 2022. It will now be able to expand its coverage as a result of commonwealth funding, and do so permanently. We know that it’s really important to work with people offending or at risk of offending, so we can intervene earlier, and ultimately k

stories.title: Iran war: US military to blockade Iranian ports
stories.summary: President Donald Trump wants to use the blockade to stop Iran from collecting transit fees and to cut off its oil revenue. Oil prices rose sharply after the announcement. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.title: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Iran warns move would breach ceasefire as US president also repeats threats to strike critical infrastructure Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has said the US will begin blockading the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to take control of the strategic waterway from Iran in the aftermath of failed peace negotiations between the countries in Pakistan. The US president also threatened to bomb Iran’s water treatment facilities, power plants and bridges if Tehran did not agree to abandon i

stories.title: Israeli strike kills paramedic, says Lebanese Red Cross – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our live coverage continues here A post about an hour ago on the Israel Defense Forces Telegram channel claimed that overnight, the IDF “identified a rocket launcher positioned and ready to launch toward the State of Israel in the area of Jouaiyya in southern Lebanon”. Shortly after the identification, the launcher was struck and dismantled in a rapid closure cycle, thwarting the launch before it could be carried out. Continue reading...

stories.title: More than a fifth of UK’s ‘austerity children’ scarred by poverty, study says
stories.summary: Researchers say hardship is a direct legacy of welfare benefit cuts imposed by Tory governments in recent years More than a fifth of all “austerity generation” British children have been scarred by poverty for at least half their childhood, a direct legacy of the welfare benefit cuts imposed by Conservative governments in recent years, research reveals. The proportion of children born after 2013 who spent at least six of their first 11 years of life in hardship surged after ministers froze worki

stories.title: Donald Trump launches extraordinary attack on Pope Leo calling him ‘weak’ and ‘terrible’

stories.title: Eric Swalwell quits California governor race after sexual assault allegations
stories.summary: Democratic congressman, running to replace Gavin Newsom, has faced multiple accusations Representative Eric Swalwell, the Democratic frontrunner in the fiercely contested race to be governor of California, has suspended his campaign amid a series of sexual assault and misconduct allegations by a former staff member and at least three other women. The woman who worked for Swalwell said the California congressman had sexually assaulted her twice when she was too inebriated to consent, according to

stories.title: €1m Picasso painting to be won for €100 in charity raffle
stories.summary: Number of tickets to win Tête de Femme will be capped at 120,000 and proceeds will go to Alzheimer’s research A raffle in France is offering the chance to win a portrait by Pablo Picasso for the price of a €100 (£87) ticket, with proceeds going to Alzheimer’s research. Picasso painted the gouache-on-paper Tête de Femme (Head of a Woman) in 1941. The raffle organisers’ online sales platform says the number of tickets will be capped at 120,000, meaning the draw could net €12m if they are all sold.

stories.title: Iran war could plunge 32 million into poverty, says United Nations
stories.summary: ‘Development in reverse’ taking place involving rising energy and food costs and weaker economic growth More than 32 million people worldwide could be plunged into poverty by the economic fallout from the Iran war, with developing countries expected to be hit hardest. In a report issued amid doubts over a fragile ceasefire, the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) said the world was facing a “triple shock” involving energy, food and weaker economic growth. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nigerian airstrike targeting jihadists reportedly kills at least 100 civilians

Officials confirmed a misfire had occurred but did not provide details. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why Africa remains divided over Macky Sall's UN bid

stories.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
stories.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

stories.title: Hacking women's political underrepresentation, Dutch-style

stories.title: At least 30 killed in crush at historic fortress in Haiti

stories.title: China boosts ties with Taiwan after opposition leader visits
stories.summary: China said it would resume flights between Taiwan and more cities on the mainland, as well as easing bans on Taiwanese food and TV shows.

stories.summary: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

stories.title: India news: Jaishankar meets UAE deputy PM to discuss Iran war
stories.summary: India is reaching out to its key economic partners in the Middle East to take stock of the impact of the Iran war and ensure smooth trade. Meanwhile, PM Modi is busy campaigning in West Bengal. DW has more.

stories.title: Still in shock, Beirut tries to heal after deadly Israeli attacks
stories.summary: BBC Middle East Correspondent Hugo Bachega hears from people affected by this week's attacks.

stories.title: US and Iranian officials begin high-stakes peace talks in Pakistan to resolve war
stories.summary: Three party talks with the US, Iran and Pakistan have begun after a reduction in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and other preconditions being met, according to Iran's IRNA news agency.

stories.title: Moment Artemis II capsule splashes down in Pacific
stories.summary: The Artemis II crew splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the west coast of the United States.

stories.title: Is Iran unable to locate its mines in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The New York Times reports, citing officials, that Iran may not have accurate records of the locations of all the mines it has laid. Reports suggest that the planting was carried out in a random and disorganised.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia exchange prisoners ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: The warring sides exchanged 175 prisoners of war each in a mediation facilitated by the United Arab Emirates, officials from both nations said.

stories.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan civil societies hold new round of broad talks to advance peace process
stories.summary: Armenian and Azerbaijan’s representatives met for the fourth round of talks as part of the so-called Peace Bridge Initiative to advance the historic peace process between the two South Caucasus neighbours.

stories.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
stories.summary: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.summary: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Trump threatens US 'blockade' of Strait of Hormuz after Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan fail
stories.summary: Trump said in a social media post he has “instructed our Navy to seek and interdict every vessel in International Waters that has paid a toll to Iran. No one who pays an illegal toll will have safe passage on the high seas”.

stories.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
stories.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

stories.title: Trump ‘reaping bitter fruit’ of thinking Iran intervention as easy as Venezuela, says former diplomat
stories.summary: John Feeley says US president was ‘flush with victory’ of Maduro capture and could make same mistake in Cuba Donald Trump is “reaping the bitter fruit” of erroneously thinking that the capture of Venezuela’s president, Nicolás Maduro, offered a blueprint for toppling the Iranian regime, according to one of the US state department’s most respected former Latin America experts. John Feeley, a Marine helicopter pilot who later served as the US ambassador to Panama, believed Trump had been “flush wi

stories.title: Concerns raised about motorbike tourist trail after death of British teenager in Vietnam
stories.summary: Local people say road conditions are rugged and weather unpredictable, while some say it has become too congested The recent death of a British gap-year student on the Ha Giang loop , a popular motorcycle tour through the mountains in north Vietnam, has heightened concerns about a trail reputed to be one of the most dangerous in the country. Orla Wates, 19, from Surrey, was riding as a pillion passenger when she fell off and was hit by an oncoming truck , according to local media. She was taken

stories.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
stories.summary: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

stories.title: Ukrainians shot down Iran's drones in the Gulf — what does Kyiv get in return?
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president announced that Ukrainian experts in the Middle East have already shot down Iranian drones in “several countries” with domestically produced interceptors.

stories.title: Druzhba oil pipeline will be fixed this spring, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president said the Druzhba pipeline, whose operational status remains tied to EU funding for Kyiv, will be fixed later this spring, with repair work under way.

stories.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patron The Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded. The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian shelling in Ukraine, Artemis’s lunar flyby and World Press Photo winners – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: New Zealand’s North Island braces for Cyclone Vaianu with thousands ordered to evacuate
stories.summary: Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 kmh (80 mph), is expected to hit on Sunday Thousands of New Zealanders were ordered to evacuate their homes on Saturday as the country’s North Island braced for Cyclone Vaianu, which authorities warned could cause coastal flooding and landslides. Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 km/h (80 mp/h), was expected to hit on Sunday, then pass west of the remote Chatham Islands on Monday, the country’s weather forecast

stories.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success
stories.summary: Having received assurances from Singapore over refined fuels, diesel supply will surely be next on the prime minister’s agenda Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese isn’t coming back from Singapore with a shipload of diesel in his checked baggage. That doesn’t mean his whistle-stop visit wasn’t a success, or that it won’t be seen in future as a pivotal moment if fuel stocks continue to be choked by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The government ne

stories.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
stories.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

stories.title: Germany: Police suspect antisemitism in Israeli restaurant attack in Munich
stories.summary: Police and restaurant owners believe antisemitism motivated the Munich attack. Nobody was hurt, but three windows were broken, causing damage worth several thousand euros. This comes amid tension over the war in Iran.

stories.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
stories.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

stories.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
stories.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

stories.title: Pakistan prepares to host US-Iran talks as ceasefire remains shaky
stories.summary: Islamabad is getting ready to host talks on Friday between the United States and Iran on a resolution to the war and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains at a standstill despite the ceasefire.

stories.title: Pentagon denies threatening Vatican over pope's remarks on US intervention in Iran
stories.summary: Pentagon and Vatican envoys have both denied reports that US officials gave the Holy See's ambassador to the US a "bitter lecture," demanding the Church align with the Trump administration over Pope Leo XIV's criticism of the Iran war.

stories.title: Overlooked IRGC institution holds key to Iran's Hormuz approach, expert warns
stories.summary: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire is already faltering, with Tehran still blocking the Strait of Hormuz navigation in protest against Israel’s attacks in Lebanon. A leading Iran expert indicates Tehran's Hormuz strategy could be part of a well-established strategy.

stories.title: Israeli restaurant targeted in suspected antisemitic attack in Munich
stories.summary: Antisemitic offences in Germany have risen significantly in recent years, reaching a new peak in 2024. Authorities recorded 6,236 cases, including 173 violent incidents, according to available data.

stories.title: Mr Nobody on leaving Russia behind: Oscar-winning director Pavel Talankin talks home truths
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview, Paval Talankin, winner of the 2026 Best Documentary Oscar, tells Euronews about live outside his Russian homeland and the "discarded" generation for whom he made 'Mr Nobody Against Putin'.

stories.title: Ongoing wars 'not failure of UN but of member states', General Assembly President tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the United Nations' main policy-making organ responded to mounting criticism of her organisation in a Euronews interview, arguing the abuse of vetoes by key members is blocking progress.

stories.title: Former hostage In Iran faces surreal tax troubles in France after release
stories.summary: Former French hostage in Iran, Benjamin Brière, said his release did not end his ordeal, as he struggled with taxes, benefits and reintegration back home.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron meets Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican to discuss global tensions
stories.summary: France’s President Emmanuel Macron met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Friday, marking their first official talks and signalling renewed dialogue between France and the Holy See at a time of rising global tensions.

stories.title: Israel: Hezbollah rocket from Lebanon strikes Safed, injuring several people
stories.summary: Emergency services were deployed in Safed on 10 April 2026 after a rocket fired from Lebanon by Hezbollah struck the northern city, prompting a rapid response to treat casualties and secure the area.

stories.title: Italy: From Go to Monopoly, board games bridge generations
stories.summary: A new exhibition titled “0–99. Design for Play” opened on 10 April 2026 at Palazzo Arese Borromeo in Cesano Maderno, near Milan, turning a 17th‑century aristocratic residence into a large, interactive gallery of games.

stories.title: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The US vice president said he was "looking forward to negotiations" in Pakistan as UK Prime Minister Starmer classified the ceasefire as "fragile".

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II astronauts back on earth after record-breaking trip around the moon
stories.summary: With their flight now complete, the four astronauts have set NASA up for a moon landing by another crew in just two years and a full-blown moon base within the decade.
